title: Dollar Tree, Inc. (DLTR) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Dollar Tree, Inc. (DLTR) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A modest 4% growth rate reflects the ongoing struggles with the Family Dollar turnaround and macroeconomic pressures on their core demographic. While the main Dollar Tree banner is stable, overall free cash flow growth will be constrained by necessary capital expenditures to modernize stores and fix logistics.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.5% discount rate accounts for the heightened execution risk surrounding the Family Dollar segment, balanced against the relatively recession-resistant nature of the core discount retail business.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.0% terminal growth rate aligns with long-term inflation targets and reflects a mature retail footprint with limited avenues for explosive long-term expansion beyond incremental store additions.

thesisParagraphs: Dollar Tree's valuation is entirely dependent on its ability to resolve the anchor that is Family Dollar. The core Dollar Tree concept remains a viable, cash-generating business, particularly after breaking the $1 barrier to alleviate margin pressure. However, the consistent underperformance of Family Dollar drags down consolidated results and requires significant capital to attempt a turnaround.

My DCF model is cautious. It assumes that while free cash flow will remain positive, growth will be severely hampered by the need to heavily reinvest in struggling stores or absorb the costs of strategic divestitures. Until management proves they can sustainably fix or shed the underperforming assets, the stock commands a higher risk premium and lacks the robust growth required to justify current multiples.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via Finviz (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why a low 4% growth rate for a discount retailer?
faq.answer: While discount retail can be defensive, Dollar Tree is weighed down by its Family Dollar acquisition. Significant capital must be diverted to fixing supply chain issues and modernizing stores, limiting the free cash flow available to grow aggressively.

faq.question: Does this valuation account for a spin-off of Family Dollar?
faq.answer: The model prices the company as it exists today. A successful spin-off could unlock value and fundamentally alter the free cash flow trajectory of the remaining core business, potentially making this valuation conservative.

faq.question: Is this financial advice?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is a demonstration of AI reasoning based on a specific set of inputs and rigid formulas. It is not financial advice.
